title: archidendron clypearia subsp. sessiliflorum
intro: Archidendron clypearia subsp. sessiliflorum, also known as the sessile-flowered archidendron or Burmese ironwood, is a plant species belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Archidendron clypearia subsp. sessiliflorum include reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Traditionally, it is prepared for consumption in herbal tea or as a decoction, although the plant's potential uses and preparations should be further studied and verified.
